description: Analyze the human telomeric G-quadruplex (PDB: 1KF1) to visualize the potassium-coordinated guanine tetrads and extract exact K-O6 coordination distances. Fetch the structure from the RCSB PDB over the network. Identify the DNA guanine bases forming the stacked tetrads (residue name DG) and the centrally bound potassium ions (residue name K). Select all guanine O6 oxygen atoms (atom name O6) that are coordinating the potassium ions (within a 3.5 Angstrom radius). Create a visualization that clearly shows the DNA backbone, the guanine bases forming the tetrads (as sticks), and the potassium ions (as spheres). Save a high-resolution PNG figure to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/g_quadruplex.png. Finally, write a report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/k_coordination_report.txt containing: a brief summary of how many potassium ions are bound in the central channel, a list of the coordinating guanine residues (by residue number), and at least 8 explicit distance measurements between a potassium ion and a guanine O6 atom (in Angstroms).
